key_insight: IA-NNP learns interface-aware Newton preconditioning to improve convergence on difficult cohesive-zone increments without changing the underlying CZM solution set.
engineering_relevance: It helps implicit fracture and delamination simulations recover from softening-induced solver failures while preserving the original traction–separation physics.
method: Transforms manual Newton modification into a learned, state-dependent interface correction.
Uses interface graph features from opening, traction, tangent, damage/history, mixity, residuals, and neighbors.
Provides two modes: IA-NNP-Init for initial-guess lifting and IA-NNP-NL for nonlinear right preconditioning.
evidence_signal: Benchmarks on horizontal, circular, two-interface, and active-front cases show better difficult-increment convergence, branch recovery, and fewer failures than standard Newton and manual NR modification.
watch_item: The correction is bounded and confidence-gated, so performance depends on reliable gating and careful integration with the original nonlinear solver.
paper_title: Interface-Aware Neural Newton Preconditioning for Robust Cohesive Zone Model Simulations
